summary: Full end-to-end test of Student Compass MVP. All 8 backend pytest tests passed (campuses, chat, cross-campus, history, saved-resources CRUD, empty-message validation). All frontend critical flows passed via Playwright (landing, campus switcher navigation, JJ Resource Center, suggestion tiles, chat AI reply with sources, save resource, sidebar appearance, new conversation reset). No console errors. Real Claude/Firecrawl/Tavily integrations all working.

issues: Clicking 'New conversation' resets chatStarted=false which hides the sidebar entirely, so previously saved resources and recent queries are no longer visible until the user sends another chat. Minor UX inconsistency — sidebar should arguably remain visible after at least one chat in the session.

action_items: Optional UX: keep Sidebar visible after first chat even after 'New conversation' so saved resources/recent stay accessible (toggle via chatStarted-once flag or always-on after history exists).
Optional: small typo on landing right-panel sample reply has nested <strong> ('Mar 14 in Haaren Hall, Room **L.61**') which renders fine but is double-bolded markup.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: detect_cross_campus uses word-boundary on c['short'].lower() — words like 'city' and 'york' are common English words and could falsely trigger redirects (e.g., 'How do I get to New York?' on a non-CCNY campus could match 'york' or 'city'). Consider scoring/exclusion list or matching 'York College'/'CCNY' variants only.

server.py: gather_context truncates each scrape to 3500 chars and concatenates up to 4 sources -> ~14k chars context. Acceptable but could exceed token budget on edge cases — consider trimming further or summarising.
server.py uses on_event('shutdown') which is deprecated in newer FastAPI; switch to lifespan context manager for forward compat.
ChatMessage.renderMarkdown uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML on AI output. Although input is HTML-escaped first, it's still safer to use a vetted sanitizer (DOMPurify) or a markdown lib (react-markdown).
saveResource truncates content to 600 chars in handleSave — acceptable, but no indication to user when truncation occurs.
No rate limiting / auth on /api/chat — public endpoint hits paid Firecrawl+Claude on every call; add a simple throttle or session check before going to production.
seed_data_creation: Test session id TEST_<uuid> created; one saved resource created and then deleted as part of CRUD test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers campuses/chat/history/saved CRUD with real Claude+Firecrawl+Tavily; CHAT_TIMEOUT is 120s. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from frontend/.env. Browser session id stored in localStorage as sc_session_id. Sidebar only appears after chatStarted=true (first chat), and resets on 'New conversation'.
